question.text: Which of the following investment products offers tax benefits under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** ELSS is the only mutual fund category that qualifies for tax deduction under Section 80C, with a mandatory 3-year lock-in period. Other fund categories listed do not offer 80C benefits. **The Trap:** Candidates sometimes confuse ELSS with other equity funds. Only ELSS has the specific tax benefit under 80C. The 3-year lock-in is the shortest among 80C instruments. **Cross-Exam Context:** ELSS competes with PPF (15-year lock-in), NSC (5-year), and tax-saving FD (5-year) for Section 80C investments, but has the shortest lock-in at 3 years. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Position ELSS as a dual-benefit product — tax savings plus equity market participation. The 3-year lock-in enforces investment discipline and is shorter than most alternatives.
